title: DAG execution engine
text: AgentFlow Flow orchestrator runs a directed acyclic graph of AsyncNodes with topological sort, supporting both serial and concurrent dependency-ready scheduling via FuturesUnordered with max_concurrency.

title: ReAct agent runtime
text: The ReActAgent in agentflow-agents observes context, plans, calls tools, processes results, reflects, and produces a final answer, with structured AgentStep and AgentEvent emitted at each phase.

title: Plan-Execute agent
text: PlanExecuteAgent decomposes a goal into a structured plan as JSON and then sequentially executes each step. It can fall back to prompt parsing when native tool calling is unavailable.

title: Multi-agent supervisors
text: AgentFlow ships handoff, blackboard, and debate supervisors. Handoff coordinates a chain of agents through a shared HandoffTool; blackboard shares a key-value workspace; debate runs proposals plus a final judge.

title: Native LLM tool calling
text: The agentflow-llm crate exposes ToolSpec, ToolChoice, and ToolCallRequest so requests/responses carry tool calls as a first-class field. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, StepFun, Moonshot, and Mock providers all support native tool calling.

title: W3C trace context propagation
text: AgentFlow injects a W3C traceparent header into outbound LLM HTTP calls when an active LlmTraceContext scope is set. This keeps OpenTelemetry traces continuous across the agent → LLM hop.

title: OpenTelemetry span attributes
text: agentflow-tracing maps execution spans to standard GenAI attributes: gen_ai.system, gen_ai.request.model, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens, gen_ai.usage.output_tokens, alongside agentflow-specific ids.

title: macOS sandbox-exec backend
text: On macOS, the OS-level sandbox backend wraps shell and script tool commands with sandbox-exec and a generated SBPL profile that denies everything by default and only allows declared file-read, file-write, network, and process-exec capabilities.

title: Linux seccomp backend
text: On Linux, the sandbox backend installs a seccomp BPF filter via Command::pre_exec. Missing capabilities translate into kernel-enforced denial of socket/connect/bind, unlinkat/renameat, and similar syscalls.

title: Capability merge algorithm
text: Effective capabilities are computed as the four-way intersection of tool_required, skill security, tool policy allowlist, and CLI flags. Each layer is auditable and the trace records which layer dropped a capability.

title: Checkpoint round-trip schema
text: FlowValue serializes with a stable schema so File and Url variants survive a checkpoint write and reload, preserving downstream node type expectations after a failed-and-resumed run.

title: Workflow expression engine
text: run_if and while.condition expressions are parsed and evaluated by a small expression engine supporting boolean operators, comparisons, arithmetic, and helpers like len and contains over the workflow state pool.

title: Skill packages
text: Agent skills are declared via SKILL.md plus an optional skill.toml manifest. The SkillBuilder wires persona, model, tools, knowledge, memory, MCP servers, and security policy into a runnable agent.

title: Model Context Protocol integration
text: The agentflow-mcp crate provides client, server, and stdio transport for JSON-RPC 2.0 with retry, timeout, and reconnect, and an adapter that surfaces remote MCP tools through the unified ToolRegistry.

title: RAG pipeline
text: agentflow-rag implements document chunking, embedding generation through OpenAI/StepFun APIs or local ONNX, vector storage in Qdrant, similarity retrieval, BM25 keyword search, hybrid fusion, and MMR reranking.

title: Crab anatomy
text: Crabs are decapod crustaceans of the infraorder Brachyura. They have a flat body covered by a thick exoskeleton, a pair of chelae used for grasping, and a reduced abdomen tucked beneath the cephalothorax.
